file_name: SmartRxSystemsInc_20180914_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11351705_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA, and Exclusive Distribution opportunities in all other states (with either the Company or Distributor), except the state of Florida. 2. APPOINTMENT AND ACCEPTANCE: 2.1 Exclusive Appointment. Company hereby appoints Distributor as Company's exclusive Distributor for the State of Texas, and Distributor accepts the appointment and agrees to represent the Products within the Territory. Company agrees that no other Distributor will be appointed in any other state as a Distributor unless it is either the Company or Distributor, save and except for the state of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA, and Exclusive Distribution opportunities in all other states (with either the Company or Distributor), except the state of Florida. 2. APPOINTMENT AND ACCEPTANCE: 2.1 Exclusive Appointment. Company hereby appoints Distributor as a non-exclusive Distributor for the State of Texas, and Distributor accepts the appointment and agrees to represent the Products within the Territory. Company agrees that additional Distributors will be appointed in Texas as the Company sees fit to meet market demand.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the Distributor has exclusive rights in Texas. The added text explicitly appoints the Distributor as a 'non-exclusive Distributor' and allows the Company to appoint 'additional Distributors in Texas,' directly contradicting the exclusivity clause of the original agreement. This introduces in-text contradiction within section 2.1 by using term 'exclusive Distributor' and 'non-exclusive Distributor' at the same time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. APPOINTMENT AND ACCEPTANCE: 2.1 Exclusive Appointment. Company hereby appoints Distributor as a non-exclusive Distributor for the State of Texas, and Distributor accepts the appointment and agrees to represent the Products within the Territory. Company agrees that additional Distributors will be appointed in Texas as the Company sees fit to meet market demand.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly appoints the Distributor as a 'non-exclusive Distributor' and allows the Company to appoint 'additional Distributors in Texas,' which directly contradicts the original concept of exclusivity for the State of Texas. The term 'exclusive Distributor' and 'non-exclusive Distributor' used simultaneously creates a clear in-text contradiction as they have opposite meanings.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties further agree that to the extent a Pharmacy Kiosk is located in a medical facility that is subject to the Stark Law (or similar State Stark Law), that such medical facility may not be able to fulfill any Federal Healthcare Program ("FHP") (Medicare, Medicaid, Tricare) prescription, but rather the Pharmacy Kiosk will bill such FHP prescriptions through a separate billing system. The Parties cannot share such FHP revenues with the Healthcare Facility Investors who are subject to the applicable Stark Laws, but Company and A3 Development Group, LLC shall share in the EBITDA on a 60/40 basis respectfully.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties further agree that to the extent a Pharmacy Kiosk is located in a medical facility that is subject to the Stark Law (or similar State Stark Law), that such medical facility may not be able to fulfill any Federal Healthcare Program ("FHP") (Medicare, Medicaid, Tricare) prescription, but rather the Pharmacy Kiosk will bill such FHP prescriptions through a separate billing system. The Parties must share such FHP revenues with the Healthcare Facility Investors who are subject to the applicable Stark Laws, but Company and A3 Development Group, LLC shall share in the EBITDA on a 60/40 basis respectfully.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the parties *cannot* share FHP revenues with Healthcare Facility Investors. The changed text states that the parties *must* share such revenues. This introduces a direct contradiction regarding compliance with Stark Law, creating uncertainty and potential legal issues about revenue distribution. This introduces in-text contradiction within section 4.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Payment. Payment to the Distributor for the sale of the Products to Customers shall be as follows: (i) Company will pay Distributor (or its Assignee) Ten Percent (10%) of the Master Distributor Override generated by each Customer Kiosk Location. (Master Distributor Override ("MDO") shall mean Total Revenue per Customer Kiosk Location collected by SRXS from third party payors and patient cash payments). This 10% of the total revenue generated is payable only if the EBITDA is a positive EBITDA and exceeds the 10% of the revenue. In cases of a positive EBITDA but shortage of funds compared to the 10% of the revenue, Distributor will take less than 10%. If the EBITDA is negative, no payments will be made to Distributor. Additionally, it will be the sole responsibility of Distributor to make a full disclosure of the 10% MDO to all its clients. (ii) The MDO will be payable within ninety (90) days from the date of the transaction (First Month payable by the end of ninety (90) days from the end of the month). (iii) For each Customer Kiosk contracted and implemented by Distributor, Company shall also pay a management fee of 40% of Net Income (Net Income for this purpose is defined as EBITDA less percentage paid to the client. The balance is then split 40%/60% to A3 Development Group, LLC and Smart RX Systems, Inc. respectively). Net Income is defined as total Customer revenue collections less all incurred expenses, and is the distributable cash generated by each Customer location. These payments will also follow the payment policy outlined in 4.1 above. (iv) The Payment(s) to Distributor or its Assignee, as set forth herein, shall be further set forth in each Class A Series Agreement, along with the respective ownership interest for both the Company and the Distributor (or its Assignee) for each respective Kiosk Location which shall have its own separate Series, as defined by the Texas Business Organizations Code for Series Limited Liability Companies, and as set forth in the Company's Operating Agreement. (v) The Payments set forth in Section 4.1(i) and 4.1(iii) above will reflect the following: The Parties further agree that to the extent a Pharmacy Kiosk is located in a medical facility that is subject to the Stark Law (or similar State Stark Law), that such medical facility may not be able to fulfill any Federal Healthcare Program ("FHP") (Medicare, Medicaid, Tricare) prescription, but rather the Pharmacy Kiosk will bill such FHP prescriptions through a separate billing system. The Parties must share such FHP revenues with the Healthcare Facility Investors who are subject to the applicable Stark Laws, but Company and A3 Development Group, LLC shall share in the EBITDA on a 60/40 basis respectfully.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within section 4.1. The original text stated the parties 'cannot' share FHP revenues, whereas the changed text states the parties 'must' share such revenues. This direct conflict introduces in-text contradiction by creating incompatible statements in Section 4.1, regarding compliance with Stark Law, leading to uncertainty about revenue distribution and making the contract's obligations unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Termination by Company under this Section 12.2 shall be effective sixty (60) days following Company's giving of notice to Distributor if the occurrence giving rise to the right of termination has not been cured, or immediately in the event of a breach of Section 6 regarding Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information or Section 7.1 regarding conduct injurious to Company's reputation.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination by Company under this Section 12.2 shall be effective immediately following Company's giving of notice to Distributor, regardless of the nature of the breach or whether a cure period is applicable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a sixty-day cure period for most breaches before termination takes effect and offers an immediate termination right for breaches to certain sections. The changed text removes the cure period and allows for immediate termination for all breaches. This in-text contradiction will be creating issues and uncertainty for Distributor. This introduces in-text contradiction within section 12.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 Termination for Cause. In addition to termination under Section 12.1, either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice to the other party: (i) if the other party fails to perform or comply with this Agreement or any provision thereof (subject to the cure period described below); or (ii) if the other party becomes insolvent, admits in writing its inability to pay its debts as they mature, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or becomes the subject of a petition under any bankruptcy act. Termination by Company under this Section 12.2 shall be effective immediately following Company's giving of notice to Distributor, regardless of the nature of the breach or whether a cure period is applicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 12.2 eliminates the cure period for breaches, making termination effective immediately for all breaches. The contradicted text, which is actually the same section, contains the original language with a cure period for breaches, which directly conflicts with the changed version which states immediate termination regardless of the breach. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the conditions for termination, making the obligations unclear. This corresponds to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition, since it's the same clause that has conflicting implications.
